data.current_description: Set a feature flag for an app. For example, update the `hs-hide-crm-cards` flag's `defaultState` to `ON` to hide classic CRM cards from new installs.
data.description_suggestion: Set or update a feature flag for an app by specifying the flag name and desired state. Accepts a FlagPutRequest body containing the flag configuration (e.g., setting `hs-hide-crm-cards` defaultState to `ON` to hide classic CRM cards from new installs). Returns the updated FlagResponse with the new flag state. This operation creates the flag if it doesn't exist or updates it if it already exists.
data.reasoning: The current description provides a helpful example but lacks clarity about input requirements (FlagPutRequest structure), output format (FlagResponse), and side effects (create vs. update behavior). Enhanced description specifies what the request body should contain, what is returned, and clarifies the idempotent nature of the operation for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'delete-/feature-flags/v3/{appId}/flags/{flagName}' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description lacks information about the response/return value and the permanent nature of the deletion (side effects). Enhanced description clarifies that deletion is permanent, specifies the success response code, and emphasizes the irreversible nature to help AI agents understand the consequences of this operation.

data.current_description: Delete a feature flag in an app.  For example, delete the `hs-release-app-cards` flag after all accounts have been migrated.
data.description_suggestion: Delete a feature flag from an app by specifying the app ID and flag name. This operation permanently removes the flag configuration and cannot be undone. Returns a 200 status on successful deletion. Use this operation after the flag is no longer needed across all accounts (e.g., after `hs-release-app-cards` migration is complete).
data.reasoning: The current description lacks information about the response/return value and the permanent nature of the deletion (side effects). Enhanced description clarifies that deletion is permanent, specifies the success response code, and emphasizes the irreversible nature to help AI agents understand the consequences of this operation.

data.current_description: Set the portal flag state for multiple HubSpot accounts at once. Use this endpoint to manage flag exposure for groups of HubSpot accounts.
data.description_suggestion: Set or update the portal flag state for multiple HubSpot accounts in a single request. This endpoint allows batch management of flag exposure across groups of accounts. Accepts a batch of account-flag state pairs and returns the results of each upsert operation. Existing flag states will be overwritten with the provided values.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what is returned and the upsert behavior (create or update). Added details about batch processing semantics, return value, and side effects to improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.
